title: laggera brevipes
intro: Laggera brevipes, also known as African tea or bush tea, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various health conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and wounds. The health benefits of Laggera brevipes include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are often made into a tea, and the roots are used to prepare a decoction, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
